title: Identifying Your Dog's Motivators and Reward Hierarchy

cues.label: Identifying Motivators
cues.headline: What makes your individual dog excited?
cues.narration: We start by discovering what truly excites your unique dog, as motivators vary.

cues.label: Observation: Enthusiasm & Focus
cues.headline: Watch for enthusiasm, speed, and sustained focus.
cues.narration: Observe how quickly your dog responds to different items and how focused they remain.

cues.label: Building a Reward Hierarchy
cues.headline: Categorize rewards from low- to high-value.
cues.narration: Create a mental list of rewards, ranking them by how much your dog desires them.

cues.label: Examples: Low to High Value
cues.headline: Kibble, training treats, boiled chicken.
cues.narration: From dry kibble to special cooked meats, rewards have different levels of appeal.

cues.label: Strategic Reward Deployment
cues.headline: Match reward value to task difficulty and distraction.
cues.narration: Use high-value rewards for challenging tasks in distracting environments, and lower-value for easy tasks.

cues.label: Influencing Motivation
cues.headline: Tailored rewards boost focus and willingness to learn.
cues.narration: By tailoring rewards, you directly influence your dog's motivation, helping them focus and overcome challenges.
